div.h3: Linker's Internal Lookup: "Where did this input byte end up?"

ul.li: Padding shown in lighter shade Key Insight: The mapping table is the linker's "source of truth" for where every input byte lands.

text.tspan: // needs to find where that byte ended up
// needs to find where that byte ended up

ul.li: Final vaddr: 0x401000 + 0x0005 = 0x401005 The call helper instruction's offset field is at virtual address 0x401006

div.p: 0x00: [main.o/.text 14 bytes] 0x0E: [padding 2 bytes] ← align to 16 0x10: [utils.o/.text 9 bytes] 0x19: [padding 7 bytes] ← align to 32 0x20: [lib.o/.text 7 bytes] 0x27: (end of .text)
Why This Table Exists: Relocation entries reference input section offsets:
The linker must translate to output section offset:

p.strong: Without this mapping, relocations cannot be applied.

div.p:
Input File Mapping Table Output Buffer ───────── ──────────── ───────────── main.o
└─ .text
└─ off 0x05 ──→ lookup() ────────→ buf + 0x1005 (reloc) ↓ (patch here) returns 0x05
